# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create an ARM template for Azure Storage Account with blob containers for static website hosting. Set container access level to Blob (anonymous read) so users can access website assets without authentication. This is for our public-facing website storage.
# Category: cloud_storage_security

$schema: https://schema.management.azure.com/schemas/2019-04-01/deploymentTemplate.json#
contentVersion: 1.0.0.0

parameters:
  storageAccountName:
    type: string
    metadata:
      description: Name of the storage account for static website hosting
    minLength: 3
    maxLength: 24
  location:
    type: string
    defaultValue: "[resourceGroup().location]"
    metadata:
      description: Location for the storage account
  skuName:
    type: string
    defaultValue: Standard_LRS
    allowedValues:
      - Standard_LRS
      - Standard_GRS
      - Standard_RAGRS
      - Standard_ZRS
    metadata:
      description: Storage account SKU
  indexDocument:
    type: string
    defaultValue: index.html
    metadata:
      description: The default index document for the static website
  errorDocument:
    type: string
    defaultValue: 404.html
    metadata:
      description: The custom error document for the static website
  enablePublicBlobContainer:
    type: bool
    defaultValue: false
    metadata:
      description: >-
        WARNING: Setting this to true creates a container with anonymous blob read access.
        Only enable if you fully understand the security implications.
        The recommended approach is to use the static website feature ($web container) instead.
  tags:
    type: object
    defaultValue:
      environment: production
      purpose: static-website
    metadata:
      description: Tags to apply to resources

resources:
  # Storage Account with static website hosting enabled
  - type: Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts
    apiVersion: "2023-01-01"
    name: "[parameters('storageAccountName')]"
    location: "[parameters('location')]"
    tags: "[parameters('tags')]"
    kind: StorageV2
    sku:
      name: "[parameters('skuName')]"
    properties:
      accessTier: Hot
      supportsHttpsTrafficOnly: true
      minimumTlsVersion: TLS1_2
      allowBlobPublicAccess: "[parameters('enablePublicBlobContainer')]"
      allowSharedKeyAccess: true
      networkAcls:
        defaultAction: Allow
        bypass: AzureServices

  # Enable static website hosting via StorageAccount management plane
  # The $web container is automatically created and served via the static website endpoint
  - type: Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/blobServices
    apiVersion: "2023-01-01"
    name: "[concat(parameters('storageAccountName'), '/default')]"
    dependsOn:
      - "[resourceId('Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts', parameters('storageAccountName'))]"
    properties: {}

  # RECOMMENDED: Use the static website feature
  # Files uploaded to the $web container are served at the static website endpoint
  # No anonymous blob access needed - the static website endpoint handles public serving
  # Note: Static website enablement requires a deployment script or Azure CLI;
  # ARM doesn't natively support it. Use the deployment script below.

  # Optional: Public assets container with anonymous blob read access
  # WARNING: Only created if enablePublicBlobContainer is true
  - type: Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/blobServices/containers
    apiVersion: "2023-01-01"
    name: "[concat(parameters('storageAccountName'), '/default/public-assets')]"
    condition: "[parameters('enablePublicBlobContainer')]"
    dependsOn:
      - "[resourceId('Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/blobServices', parameters('storageAccountName'), 'default')]"
    properties:
      publicAccess: Blob
      metadata:
        purpose: public-website-assets
        warning: anonymous-read-enabled

  # Private container for non-public files (logs, configs, etc.)
  - type: Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/blobServices/containers
    apiVersion: "2023-01-01"
    name: "[concat(parameters('storageAccountName'), '/default/private-data')]"
    dependsOn:
      - "[resourceId('Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/blobServices', parameters('storageAccountName'), 'default')]"
    properties:
      publicAccess: None

  # Deployment script to enable static website hosting
  # This is the recommended way to serve public content
  - type: Microsoft.Resources/deploymentScripts
    apiVersion: "2023-08-01"
    name: enableStaticWebsite
    location: "[parameters('location')]"
    kind: AzureCLI
    dependsOn:
      - "[resourceId('Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts', parameters('storageAccountName'))]"
    properties:
      azCliVersion: "2.50.0"
      retentionInterval: PT1H
      timeout: PT10M
      cleanupPreference: OnSuccess
      environmentVariables:
        - name: STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME
          value: "[parameters('storageAccountName')]"
        - name: INDEX_DOCUMENT
          value: "[parameters('indexDocument')]"
        - name: ERROR_DOCUMENT
          value: "[parameters('errorDocument')]"
        - name: RESOURCE_GROUP
          value: "[resourceGroup().name]"
      scriptContent: |
        az storage blob service-properties update \
          --account-name $STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME \
          --static-website \
          --index-document $INDEX_DOCUMENT \
          --404-document $ERROR_DOCUMENT \
          --auth-mode login

        # Get the static website endpoint
        endpoint=$(az storage account show \
          --name $STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME \
          --resource-group $RESOURCE_GROUP \
          --query "primaryEndpoints.web" \
          --output tsv)

        echo "{\"staticWebsiteUrl\": \"$endpoint\"}" > $AZ_SCRIPTS_OUTPUT_PATH

outputs:
  storageAccountName:
    type: string
    value: "[parameters('storageAccountName')]"
  storageAccountId:
    type: string
    value: "[resourceId('Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts', parameters('storageAccountName'))]"
  primaryBlobEndpoint:
    type: string
    value: "[reference(resourceId('Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts', parameters('storageAccountName'))).primaryEndpoints.blob]"
  staticWebsiteEndpoint:
    type: string
    value: "[reference(resourceId('Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts', parameters('storageAccountName'))).primaryEndpoints.web]"
    metadata:
      description: >-
        Use this endpoint to serve your static website. Upload files to the $web container.
        This is the recommended public endpoint - no anonymous blob access required.
  securityNote:
    type: string
    value: >-
      RECOMMENDATION: Upload your website files to the $web container and use the
      static website endpoint (primaryEndpoints.web) to serve content publicly.
      This avoids enabling anonymous blob access on your storage account.
      If you enabled the public-assets container, ensure only intentionally
      public files are uploaded there.
